drive_folder_contents: 2-182 Ops Notes Rail (Google Sheet, modified today 11:00 AM)

rail_specific_calibration_from_max.max_answer: Just labeling. Signals to VVB to expect SCALE TICKETS rather than BOL (truck batches use BOL as authoritative mass; rail batches use scale tickets).
rail_specific_calibration_from_max.verification_implication: Section 9 mapping should accept either 'AECN Pyrolysis Process/Embodied' OR 'AECN pyrolysis (from rail loads)...' as valid. Mass authority on rail batches = scale tickets, not BOL.

rail_specific_calibration_from_max.max_answer: Expected. 3 legs (AECN>SOPOR, SOPOR>Opelousas, Opelousas>Basco) each with process + embodied = 6.
rail_specific_calibration_from_max.verification_implication: Standard rail structure. Watch for unit-conversion errors across the 3 legs — Max flagged unit conversions on the rail components are annoying and error-prone.

rail_specific_calibration_from_max.max_answer: Both the EF and the mileage on the rail component appear to be WRONG. Most of the evidence on the distance datapoint is also erroneous.
rail_specific_calibration_from_max.verification_implication: REAL FAIL. Need to: (a) read EF used in Certify rail components, (b) read distance value, (c) check distance datapoint linked sources, (d) compare to Standard EF Sheet rail factor and Google Maps SOPOR>Opelousas distance, (e) recompute correct values. Both rail process AND embodied likely affected.

rail_specific_calibration_from_max.max_answer: Expected. Transport process is the ONLY BCU category for typical batches (truck or rail). Other BCU categories only appear when removals are burdened with site emissions.
rail_specific_calibration_from_max.verification_implication: 1 BCU line is correct for this batch. Confirm BCU quantity matches transport process activity sum (already verified: 1.594 = 0.264 + 1.225 + 0.105).

rail_embodied_component_deep_read.component_name: SOPOR to Opelousas Rail Transport Embodied Emissions
rail_embodied_component_deep_read.calculation_formula: output = distance * carbon_intensity (no mass term in formula; mass-allocation is baked into the 'allocated distance' input)
rail_embodied_component_deep_read.arithmetic_check_OLD_METHODOLOGY: 606.2422 mi * 0.03522 kgCO2e/mi = 21.35 kgCO2e = 0.021 tCO2e (internally consistent under OLD per-mile + manually-allocated distance methodology)
rail_embodied_component_deep_read.arithmetic_check_NEW_METHODOLOGY: 606.2422 mi * 0.0311307 kgCO2e/mi = 18.87 kgCO2e = 0.01887 tCO2e (under NEWER GLEC V3.2 2025 per-mile EF, with the published unit label corrected from 'T-mi' to 'mi'). Embodied is always per-mile, never per-T-mi. Per-T-mi is only used for process emissions where fuel scales with cargo mass.
rail_embodied_component_deep_read.delta_old_vs_new: ~12% lower under newer EF (small but material)
rail_embodied_component_deep_read.convention_locked_in_lessons_learned: Embodied emissions = per-mile (or per-vehicle-lifetime amortized to per-mile). Process emissions = per-T-mi. Sheet derivation: total manufacture EE / EPA useful life miles -> g/mi -> *deadhead factor -> MTCO2e/mi. Same shape for railcar, truck, trailer.

distance_emission_factor.datapoint_name: Emissions Factor - Vehicle Embodied Emissions -- Railcar (per Standard EF Sheet)
distance_emission_factor.matches_standard_ef_sheet: Yes - matches OLDER 'Updated' entry (0.0000352240 MT CO2e/mi, source USDOT/USEPA/Cambridge Systematics) exactly. Standard EF sheet has a NEWER 'Updated' entry at 0.0000311307 MT CO2e/T-mi (GLEC V3.2 2025) which Certify is NOT using.
distance_emission_factor.RETRACTION: Originally flagged as wrong factor TYPE (truck applied to rail). RETRACTED - the datapoint is named 'Vehicle Embodied Emissions - Railcar', i.e. the railcar-specific entry from the Vehicle Embodied family. EF is correct under old methodology.
distance_emission_factor.active_issue: EF is stale per the most recent Standard EF sheet entry, which switches to per-T-mi methodology. Magnitude under new methodology would be ~70x higher.

distance_traveled.datapoint_name: Allocated Distance Sopor Quebec to Opelousas
distance_traveled.RETRACTION: Originally flagged as wrong distance (606 mi can't be Quebec-LA). RETRACTED - this is correctly an ALLOCATED distance: full SOPOR>Opelousas rail leg = 2199 mi, allocated to this batch via upstream allocation fraction = 21509/78020 = 0.2757. 2199 * 0.2757 = 606.30 mi (matches 606.2422 within rounding).
distance_traveled.ops_notes_match: Rail (Miles) column in offload_1 row of Ops Notes 2-182 = 606.2422773 - exact match.
distance_traveled.todo_optional: Open all 5 sources on this datapoint to confirm what evidence supports the full 2199 mi + the 0.2757 allocation derivation

methodology_resolution_per_max_2026_04_2.current_correct_EF: 0.0000311307 MT CO2e/mi (the newer GLEC V3.2 2025 entry, with the sheet's '/T-mi' unit label corrected to '/mi')
methodology_resolution_per_max_2026_04_2.net_impact_on_2-182: Net moves from 30.46 tCO2e to ~30.46 tCO2e (rounding) - delta is 0.07% level, immaterial at headline
methodology_resolution_per_max_2026_04_2.propagation: Same shared datapoint feeds 4 rail process components per the Usages count. Fixing the source datapoint updates all four removals using it.
methodology_resolution_per_max_2026_04_2.sheet_followup: Standard EFs sheet 2026 tab newer entry has unit label '/T-mi' that should read '/mi'. Embodied is always per-mile.
methodology_resolution_per_max_2026_04_2.v17_LCA_followup_open: Neither published value (old 0.0000352240 nor new 0.0000311307) reflects the V17 DOT-111 LCA's 65.37 MT CTGate. Implied lifetime mileage at 0.0000311307 is ~2.10M mi. Whether to re-derive cleanly against V17 + a current EPA/AAR lifetime mileage figure is a separate decision.

ops_notes_observations.this_batch_offload_position: Offload 1 of 4 - rows 2-4 are zero, suggesting 3 more batches will come from same railcar (likely 2-183, 2-184, 2-185)

distance_allocations.AECN_to_SOPOR_full_mi: 107.5 single trip x 3 trucks = 322.5 mi total

rail_process_component_deep_read.component_name: SOPOR to Opelousas Rail Transport Process Emissions

emission_factor.datapoint_full_name: Emission Factors - Rail Transport Fuel Use (Well to Wheel)
evidence_concerns: UNIT SUSPICION: GLEC V3.2 rail freight well-to-wheel EFs are typically published per t-km, not t-mi. Value of 0.0259 kgCO2e/(t-mi) is in the right magnitude range to be a t-km value (~0.022-0.030 typical for diesel rail freight in US/CA per GLEC). Need to open Standard EF sheet 2026 tab to verify whether the source value is per t-km vs per t-mi, and whether unit conversion was applied correctly.
If source is per t-km but Certify is treating as per t-mi: rail process under-counts emissions by factor of 1.609.
If source value should be 0.0259/1.609 = 0.0161 per t-mi: Certify over-counts emissions by factor of 1.609.

distance_traveled.uncertainty_justification: None (KB says distance does not require uncertainty - sensitivity flag may still trigger Certify warning)

distance_traveled.important_observation: Rail Embodied component is NOT in this datapoint's Usages list. Rail embodied uses a separate distance datapoint - need to verify whether it has the same value or differs.
distance_traveled.source_evidence_content: PNG screenshot of an email from Matt P. Bergeland (Rail Logistics Specialist, Watco, Fort Worth TX, matthew.bergeland@watco.com) addressed to Antoinette. Body text: 'Hi Antoinette, The total milage for this move was 2199. Thanks, Matt Bergeland'. No date visible in capture, no Google Maps screenshot, no rail manifest/waybill, no specification of which leg 'this move' refers to (whole AECN->Basco shipment or just SOPOR->Opelousas rail leg).
evidence_issues_RETAINED: FILENAME: Generic 'image (44).png' - not descriptive (FLAG-level hygiene issue)
evidence_issues_RETRACTED_PER_MAX: EVIDENCE TYPE: Originally flagged as wrong evidence type vs Google Maps. RETRACTED - there is no Google Maps for rail. Email from rail transport provider IS the authoritative evidence per Max.

LEG ATTRIBUTION: Originally questioned whether 'this move' covers SOPOR>Opelousas only or full route. RETRACTED - Watco IS a rail logistics company (Matt Bergeland = Rail Logistics Specialist) so 'this move' refers specifically to the rail leg they handle. 2199 mi is plausible Quebec>LA rail distance.

MISSING UNCERTAINTY JUSTIFICATION: Originally flagged that Certify shows 'No uncertainty justification' on this datapoint. KB exempts distance from uncertainty, so this is moot - dropping unless raised as 'Certify wants something the protocol does not require' hygiene note.

mass_of_load.datapoint_name: Allocated Bio-Oil Mass - AECN via Rail
mass_of_load.uncertainty_methodology_RETRACTED: Originally flagged as wrong methodology (per-truck rule applied instead of per-railcar rule). RETRACTED per Max: 3 BOLs go INTO the railcar (3-truck input uncertainty = 217.71 kg), but the railcar gets offloaded into 4 trucks OUT, each carrying its own ~72.57 kg uncertainty. Applying the per-truck-out rule across 4 trucks = 290.28 kg total batch uncertainty, slightly more conservative than the strict in-rule. Acceptable.

cobb_data.status: FOUND - row 59 in 'Basco Injection -- COBB' tab (NOT 'COBB (with offload info)' as the KB stated)
cobb_data.previous_claim_RETRACTED: Earlier in this pass I claimed COBB had not been populated. That was wrong - the Drive MCP natural-language read returned an abbreviated view that omitted the data tabs. After re-fetching as binary xlsx via download_file_content and parsing all 60+ tabs locally, the row was found in 'Basco Injection -- COBB'.
cobb_data.kb_correction_needed: KB Section on Bio-oil Injection Tracker says rail batches use 'COBB (with offload info)' tab. Actually rail batches (including this one and its sister offloads 2-183/184/185) are in 'Basco Injection -- COBB'. The 'COBB (with offload info)' tab contains older entries (max batch 2-122 / 3-54). KB should be corrected.
cobb_data.loads_tab_inbound_record: Loads tab row 270: Q1 2026, BOLs 2310-R/2312-R/2313-R, carrier Watco, asset GPRX5188, received 2026-02-27, dwell to 2026-03-16, total 78.02 MT, work order 'Rail_GPRX 5188_2.27.26'
